articleStubs.title: GitHub Vercel Labs Python Update
articleStubs.dek: Agentic Coding: RT elvisVery interesting results from this NanoGPT-Bench eval.There is so much talk about self-improving agents.But can coding agents do real AI R&D?@IntologyAI reports that Codex, Claude Code, and Autoresearch recover only 9.3% of human progress.Coding agents spend more of their compute on hyperparameter tuning.In fact, coding agents rarely attempt algorithmic research at all.Claude Code and Autoresearch both reason more about algorithmic research, but still dodge implementation.Read more : https://www.intology.ai/blog/nanogpt-benchIntology: Can coding agents do research?We release NanoGPT-Bench, an internal eval we’ve used to test agents on an AI R&D problem with months of human progressCodex, Claude Code, Autoresearch recover only 9.3% of human progress, mostly tuning hyperparams & ignoring algorithmic research

bodySections.heading: Agentic Coding Is The Central Move
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: Agentic Coding: The post Google Introduces Gemini 3.5 Flash at I/O 2026: A Faster and Cheaper Model for AI Agents and Coding appeared first on MarkTechPost .
Grok Build 0.1 is now available on .Vercel AI Gateway This is a beta coding model trained for agentic coding, currently in early access, and powers the Grok Build CLI app.

bodySections.heading: Talk About Self-Improving Agents Changes The Practical Tradeoff
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: Talk About Self-Improving Agents: RT elvisSelf-improving AI is a big deal!As a first step, I've been exploring how much of the post-training can be automated. is a first post on how I am using @FireworksAI_HQ Agent to automate LLM fine-tuning itself.
Talk About Self-Improving Agents: Microsoft Open-Sources RAMPART and Clarity to Secure AI Agents During Development The Hacker News
Talk About Self-Improving Agents: The programming language for agents

bodySections.heading: But Can Coding Agents Changes The Practical Tradeoff
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: But Can Coding Agents is framed as a cost-control problem as much as a memory problem. The source argues that eager extraction spends model calls and prompt budget on interactions before the system knows whether they will matter later.
The better operating model is selective consolidation: keep lightweight traces available, then spend heavier reasoning only when recurrence suggests the information has durable value.

bodySections.heading: To Interact With Fireworks Agent Changes The Practical Tradeoff
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: Dataset + Skill file included.For the use case, I took inspiration from @karpathy's tweet on LLM Knowledge Bases.I asked Claude Code to interact with Fireworks Agent to fine-tune a small Qwen model to get the right output style to efficiently keep growing my PaperWiki (https://x.com/omarsar0/status/2042286186920550498?s=20).

To Interact With Fireworks Agent: GitHub Confirms Hack Impacting 3,800 Internal Repositories SecurityWeek GitHub Breached — Employee Device Hack Led to Exfiltration of 3,800+ Internal Repos The Hacker News GitHub Breached via VS Code Extension | Developer Supply Chain Attack 2026 Aikido Security
To Interact With Fireworks Agent: Introducing RAMPART and Clarity: Open source tools to bring safety into Agent development workflow Microsoft

articleStubs.title: Phase Transitions in Agent Memory: Recurrent Memory
articleStubs.dek: The core idea is delayed memory consolidation for long-running LLM agents.

bodySections.heading: Recurrent Memory Pushes Beyond The Prompt Window
bodySections.intent: Explain how the source describes memory being organized or persisted.
paragraphs: That changes the agent design problem from "save everything" to "notice what keeps mattering." The transcript connects this to agents that run for hours or days, where a short context window or a plain append-only memory file can lose the history needed for coherent follow-through.

bodySections.heading: Memory Consolidation Creates The Trigger Point
bodySections.intent: Explain how the source describes memory being organized or persisted.
paragraphs: The phase-transition framing is about when an observation becomes stable enough to promote. The source describes a threshold-like trigger: once related interactions reach enough recurrence, the system consolidates them instead of leaving them as isolated fragments.

For builders, the useful takeaway is that memory quality depends on timing. Consolidating too early burns tokens and can freeze noise into the agent state; waiting for recurrence gives the system a better chance of turning repeated evidence into useful structure.

bodySections.heading: Long-Running LLM Agents Push Beyond The Prompt Window
bodySections.intent: Explain how the source describes memory being organized or persisted.
paragraphs: The transcript separates memory into semantic and episodic forms. Semantic memory captures generalized knowledge the agent should reuse, while episodic memory preserves event-like context from prior interactions.
That split matters because long-running agents need both: durable rules and concepts for reasoning, plus enough event history to remember why a task moved in a particular direction.

keyTakeaways: Today we talk about a subconscious memory in our artificial intelligence.

Then we have a phase transition threshold activated by a mathematical trigger, and we will generate something beautiful, a semantic memory and an episodic memory in a way you have never seen it before.
So, we have a brand new study May 15, 2026.

articleStubs.title: NVIDIA Nemotron Labs Diffusion Update
articleStubs.dek: NVIDIA researchers have released Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion, a language model family that unifies three decoding modes in one architecture.

bodySections.heading: A Language Model Is The Central Move
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: A Language Model: The model supports autoregressive (AR) decoding, diffusion-based parallel decoding, and self-speculation decoding.

sources.title: NVIDIA AI Releases Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion: A Tri-Mode Language Model with 6× Tokens Per Forward Over Qwen3-8B
sources.summary: NVIDIA researchers have released Nemotron-Labs-Diffusion, a language model family that unifies three decoding modes in one architecture. The model supports autoregressive (AR) decoding, diffusion-based parallel decoding, and self-speculation decoding.

articleStubs.dek: Alibaba's Qwen team has released Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash, a real-time multimodal translation model that processes audio and video simultaneously.

bodySections.heading: Real-Time Multimodal Translation Model Is The Central Move
bodySections.intent: Explain the source-backed development and its technical consequence.
paragraphs: Real-Time Multimodal Translation Model: The model covers 60 input languages and produces speech output in 29 languages at 2.8 seconds of latency.

sources.title: Alibaba Qwen Team Introduces Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash: Real-Time Multimodal Interpretation Across 60 Languages at 2.8-Second Latency
sources.summary: Alibaba's Qwen team has released Qwen3.5-LiveTranslate-Flash, a real-time multimodal translation model that processes audio and video simultaneously. The model covers 60 input languages and produces speech output in 29 languages at 2.8 seconds of latency.

articleStubs.dek: Cache Shouldn Disappear Every Time: With @novita_labs, we're sharing PegaFlow — a production-grade external KV cache service that plugs into vLLM through the external KV connector interface.PegaFlow runs as a standalone Rust daemon owning the host KV pool, SSD cache, and RDMA resources.

With @novita_labs, we're sharing PegaFlow — a production-grade external KV cache service that plugs into vLLM through the external KV connector interface.PegaFlow runs as a standalone Rust daemon owning the host KV pool, SSD cache, and RDMA resources.
